attributes.description: Alerts when the Oracle buffer cache hit ratio falls below 90%. Below 90% indicates cache thrashing and disk-bound reads.
attributes.name: [Oracle OTel] Buffer cache hit ratio low

investigation_guide.blob:
## Oracle buffer cache hit ratio low

### What fired
`1 - physical_reads/logical_reads < 0.90` over the window.

### Why it matters
Oracle's buffer cache absorbs most reads. A sub-90% hit ratio means many reads are going to disk — a significant performance regression and often a sizing problem.

### Triage
1. Check SGA sizing and automatic memory management settings.
2. Inspect queries doing many physical reads.

### Remediation
- Increase SGA / buffer cache size.
- Tune offending queries.

### Tuning
- 90% threshold; tune per environment.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
// logical_reads and physical_reads are co-located in Reads TSDB group; INCREASE for counter deltas
| WHERE oracledb.logical_reads IS NOT NULL
        AND oracledb.physical_reads IS NOT NULL
| STATS delta_physical = SUM(INCREASE(oracledb.physical_reads)),
        delta_logical = SUM(INCREASE(oracledb.logical_reads))
        BY oracledb.instance.name, host.name
| WHERE delta_logical > 0
// Hit ratio = 1 - (physical / logical); < 90% indicates buffer cache thrashing
// Adjust threshold (e.g. 0.95 for 95%) to tune sensitivity
| EVAL hit_ratio_pct = ROUND((1.0 - delta_physical / delta_logical) * 100.0, 1)
| WHERE hit_ratio_pct < 90
| SORT hit_ratio_pct ASC
